title: NDFC Switch Inventory and Lifecycle Status
exclusions: **PSIRT**, **Field Notice**, **CVE**, and **NDI advisory-window** feeds correlated to exposure windows (UC-18.4.3 — disclosure intelligence, not switch rows); **golden firmware / imagePolicy** **In-Sync** drift versus controller targets (UC-18.4.10); **intent configuration** **complianceStatus** versus templates (UC-18.4.2); **EVPN control-plane** neighbor or **CoPP** narratives (UC-18.4.5 / UC-18.4.6); estates with **no Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller** enrollment where inventory must come from **SNMP** or **CMDB** alone; **APIC**-managed **ACI** leaf inventory without **LAN Fabric** inventory API scope (cat-18.1.*).
dataSources: `index=cisco_dc` / `index=cisco_network` / `index=network_operations` / `index=cisco_operations` receiving **JSON** from **NDFC** **LAN Fabric** REST **`GET /appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/inventory/switches`** (all managed switches) and **`GET /appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics/{fabric}/inventory`** (per-fabric roster), normalized to **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:inventory`** — controller reachability and fabric summary tiles as **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:fabric_status`** — configuration and operator actions as **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:audit`** — **`facility=local6`** controller and switch syslog as **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:syslog`** — optional advisory cross-check stubs as **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:advisory`** (digest only; not a substitute for UC-18.4.3). Poll cadence typically **300–900s** per inventory scope (**one row or small JSON bundle per device per poll** after dedupe). Pair with **Splunk REST Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for Cisco **EoX** API enrichment (`cisco:eol:bulletin` elsewhere) merged via lookup. **`cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk`** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) surfaces inventory-style panels when sourcetypes align.
app: **`cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk`** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) for packaged **NDFC** dashboards and macros; **`Splunk REST API Modular Input`** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for OAuth **Bearer** pulls under **`/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/`** plus Cisco **EoX** endpoints feeding **`cisco_switch_eox_lookup`** (maintain **`local/transforms.conf`**). Use a **custom app** or **Heavy Forwarder** **Python** poller if your **TA** does not yet ship **inventory/switches** stanzas—stamp **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:inventory`** consistently.
spl:
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=network_operations OR index=cisco_operations)
(
    sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:inventory" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status"
    OR (sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:audit" AND match(lower(_raw), "inventory|switch|serial|fabric"))
)
earliest=-24h@m
| eval serial_key=upper(trim(coalesce(serialNumber, serial_number, serial, deviceSerial, chassisSerial, serialNo, "")))
| eval dev_name=trim(coalesce(hostname, host, switch_name, deviceName, devicename, nodeName, switchName, switchId, ""))
| eval fab=trim(coalesce(fabric_name, fabricName, fabric, nd_fabric, lanFabric, ""))
| eval model_key=trim(coalesce(model, deviceModel, productId, pid, product_id, partNumber, ""))
| eval role_key=lower(trim(coalesce(switchRole, switch_role, role, nodeRole, node_role, "")))
| eval release_key=trim(coalesce(release, software_version, nxos_version, imageVersion, version, sw_version, runningImage, ""))
| eval ip_key=trim(coalesce(ipAddress, mgmt_ip, mgmtIp, management_ip, v4Addr, ipv4Addr, ""))
| eval managed_key=lower(trim(coalesce(managedStatus, managed_status, adminStatus, registrationState, onboardingState, "")))
| eval oper_key=lower(trim(coalesce(operStatus, oper_status, opStatus, reachability, connStatus, healthStatus, "")))
| where isnotnull(dev_name) AND dev_name!=""
| stats latest(_time) AS last_inv_poll latest(model_key) AS model latest(serial_key) AS serial latest(release_key) AS sw_version
        latest(ip_key) AS mgmt_ip latest(role_key) AS switch_role latest(managed_key) AS managed_status
        latest(oper_key) AS oper_status dc(fab) AS fabric_dc values(fab) AS fabric_list BY dev_name
| eval fabric_name=mvjoin(mvsort(fabric_list), " | ")
| lookup cisco_switch_eox_lookup model OUTPUT eos_date ldos_date pid_normalized AS lookup_pid
| eval days_to_ldos=if(isnotnull(ldos_date), round((strptime(ldos_date,"%Y-%m-%d")-now())/86400,1), null())
| eval serial_gap=if(isnull(serial) OR serial="" OR serial="UNKNOWN",1,0)
| eval reg_risk=case(
    managed_key IN ("unmanaged","pending","stale","unknown","unclaimed") OR match(managed_key,"false|no|not.?managed"), "registration_gap",
    oper_key IN ("down","unreachable","offline","major","critical") OR match(oper_key,"fail|loss"), "reachability_alert",
    serial_gap=1, "inventory_hole",
    1=1, "healthy")
| eval lifecycle_lane=case(isnull(ldos_date) AND isnotnull(model), "eox_lookup_miss",
    isnull(ldos_date), "no_model",
    days_to_ldos<0, "past_support_window",
    days_to_ldos<90, "ldos_within_90d",
    days_to_ldos<365, "ldos_within_1y",
    1=1, "eox_clear")
| where reg_risk!="healthy" OR lifecycle_lane IN ("eox_lookup_miss","past_support_window","ldos_within_90d","ldos_within_1y","no_model")
| eval sort_key=case(lifecycle_lane="past_support_window",0, lifecycle_lane="ldos_within_90d",1, lifecycle_lane="ldos_within_1y",2, reg_risk="reachability_alert",3, 1=1,4)
| sort sort_key days_to_ldos dev_name
| head 500
description: Reconstructs and audits the **NDFC**-managed **switch inventory** from **LAN Fabric** **inventory** APIs and related controller streams: **serialNumber**, **model**/**PID**, **release** (**NX-OS** train), **ipAddress**, **switchRole**, **managedStatus**, and **operStatus**, then aligns each row to **Cisco** **Last Date of Support** / **End-of-Support** milestones via **EoX** lookups and flags **registration**/**reachability** gaps. Deliberately separate from **PSIRT** advisory feeds (**UC-18.4.3**) and **imagePolicy** **golden** compliance (**UC-18.4.10**) while still feeding the same **procurement**, **audit**, and **software** governance forums that rely on an honest rack-level device census.
value: When the fabric controller’s device table drifts from what finance and operations expect—missing **serials**, fuzzy **PID** strings, silent **unmanaged** rows, or switches inside **LDOS** windows—capacity plans, smart-net renewals, sparing, and patch waves misfire. Anchoring Splunk to **`inventory/switches`** and **`fabrics/{fabric}/inventory`** API truth plus **EoX** lookups gives **NetOps** and **asset** teams one reconciled timeline for **refresh**, **contract**, and **version** conversations without waiting for quarterly spreadsheet rescans.
implementation: Deploy **REST** pollers (**300–900s**) on **`/lan-fabric/rest/inventory/switches`** and per-fabric **`…/control/fabrics/{fabric}/inventory`**; normalize **FIELDALIAS** for **serial**, **model**, **release**, **managedStatus**; land **`cisco:ndfc:inventory`**; maintain **`cisco_switch_eox_lookup`** from **EoX** API (**1546**); schedule exception dashboards for **LDOS** **90d**/**365d**, **registration_gap**, and **inventory_hole** rows; validate counts against **NDFC** **Inventory** UI before paging.
visualization: (1) **Table** — **dev_name**, **fabric_name**, **model**, **serial**, **sw_version**, **managed_status**, **oper_status**, **lifecycle_lane**, **days_to_ldos**. (2) **Single value** — count of **past_support_window** devices. (3) **Bar** — **reg_risk** distribution (**registration_gap**, **reachability_alert**, **inventory_hole**). (4) **Timechart** — **`last_inv_poll`** age per fabric (staleness detector). (5) **Geomap** / **status** treemap (optional) — **mgmt_ip** by **fabric_name** for geographic estates.
schema: CIM **Inventory** (**device**, **serial**, **model**, **version**/package fields mapped from **sw_version**) plus **Alerts** for **registration_gap**, **reachability_alert**, **LDOS** proximity lanes when promoted through **correlation** searches or **Enterprise Security** notable contexts.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Inventory** only after **props.conf** stabilizes **serial**/**model** extractions; keep raw **`cisco:ndfc:inventory`** for API **schema** drift across **NDFC** maintenance trains.

references.title: Cisco NDFC REST API — Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller (developer)
Cisco NDFC Fabric Controller configuration guide — inventory and LAN fabric operations
Cisco Support APIs — EoX (End-of-Sale and End-of-Life)
Cisco DC Networking Application for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)

knownFalsePositives:
**inventory_poll_shard_skew** — Multi-region **NDFC** clusters or **pagination** boundaries can delay subsets of **leaf** rows until the next API **page** cycle; **suppress** **registration_gap** if **`last_inv_poll`** skew is **<15m** and **NDFC** **UI** still lists the device **In-Service**.

**supervisor_switchover_serial_flip** — **active**/**standby** rotations sometimes emit blank **serialNumber** on transient polls; require **two** consecutive **inventory_hole** samples or cross-check **`cisco:ndfc:syslog`** **switchover** arcs before paging **asset** teams.

**pid_label_variants** — **Nexus** **PID** strings from **NDFC** may omit **spare** tokens (**`-=`** optics bundles) versus corporate **CMDB**; **eox_lookup_miss** clears after you normalize **lookup_pid** in **`cisco_switch_eox_lookup`** with **`FIELDALIAS`** bridges rather than treating chassis as unknown.

**greenfield_fabric_registration** — **managedStatus** may read **pending** while **POAP** completes; tie **registration_gap** alerts to **`onboardingState`** age **>48h** or **ServiceNow** **`CHG`** closure absent.

**controlled_lab_duplicates** — **Qualification** racks reuse **hostname** patterns; dedupe **`dev_name`** with **`fabric_name`** tokens or **`mgmt_ip`** before **Sev** ladders fire on **inventory_hole** alone.

**maintenance_unreach_window** — Approved **ISSU** or **reload** tickets legitimately set **operStatus** **down**; join **`inputlookup ndfc_maintenance_allowlist.csv`** windows keyed to **fabric_name** + **dev_name**.

**eox_calendar_refresh** — **EoX** API bulk reloads can shift **LDOS** **days_to_ldos** by hours as Cisco **revises** bulletins; alert on **crossing** thresholds week-over-week, not single-minute jitter.

**audit_noise_rows** — **`cisco:ndfc:audit`** lines mentioning **inventory** during operator **export** jobs can duplicate device strings; keep primary **`stats`** on **`cisco:ndfc:inventory`** and treat **audit** as **optional** corroboration only.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **NDFC 12.x** with **LAN Fabric** licensed and reachable over HTTPS from the Splunk **Heavy Forwarder** or dedicated **REST worker**; provision an **RBAC** service account with read rights to inventory—**Network Operator**–class roles are typical, but confirm the exact label in your **Nexus Dashboard** tenant.
• Keep **NTP** aligned on collectors and controllers so _time reflects poll cadence; inventory **JSON** may carry its own lastUpdated field—prefer collector _time for **SLA** math unless Cisco documentation directs otherwise.
• Install **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**Splunkbase 1546**) or an internal Python poller that exchanges **Bearer** tokens through **ND SSO**; store credentials in Splunk credential objects instead of pasting secrets into **inputs.conf**.
• Deploy **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (**Splunkbase 7777**) when you want sample dashboards—still verify sourcetype parity with this use case.
• Build **cisco_switch_eox_lookup** (CSV plus **transforms.conf**) keyed on model and PID strings exactly as NDFC returns them; schedule a weekly refresh from **Cisco EoX API** responses at **https://api.cisco.com/software/eol/v2/lifecycle/products** and normalize into **pid_normalized** columns for stubborn **CMDB** variants.
• Provision indexes (cisco_dc is common) and Splunk roles so multi-tenant fabric operators cannot query another tenant’s inventory shard.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Primary inventory pull: schedule **GET https://<nd-host>/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/inventory/switches** every 300–900 seconds. After tuning **LINE_BREAKER** and **TIMESTAMP_EXTRACTION**, expect roughly one compact **JSON** document per managed switch per poll; set sourcetype to **cisco:ndfc:inventory** and source to ndfc:inventory:switches for traceability.
(2) Per-fabric enrichment: enumerate fabricName values from **/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics**, then call **GET …/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics/{fabric}/inventory** on staggered offsets so controller CPU stays within Cisco guidance; emit the same sourcetype with an explicit fabric field for dashboards that filter by **LAN Fabric** scope.
(3) Fabric status heartbeat: poll dashboard or summary endpoints your Cisco TA maps to **cisco:ndfc:fabric_status** so you can distinguish “controller up, inventory silent” from a full platform outage.
(4) Audit trail: stream or batch-export NDFC audit records into **cisco:ndfc:audit** for operator accountability—do not treat audit excerpts as the authoritative serial source.
(5) Syslog: forward facility=local6 lines from NDFC and switches into **cisco:ndfc:syslog** to corroborate supervisor switchovers, reloads, and transient reachability blips.
(6) Advisory digest (optional): if you must land lightweight **cisco:ndfc:advisory** **JSON** summaries, keep them as context only; PSIRT, Field Notice, and bug-match governance belong to UC-18.4.3.
Volume planning: budget one event per device per poll plus a small margin for retries; honor **HTTP 429** responses with exponential backoff per Cisco developer throttling guidance.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Authoritative SPL lives in the spl **JSON** field—paste it into Search, confirm field coverage, then layer scheduled reports (daily operational digest) and alerts (weekly procurement rollup plus immediate paging for **past_support_window** in production fabrics).

```spl
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=network_operations OR index=cisco_operations)
(
    sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:inventory" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status"
    OR (sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:audit" AND match(lower(_raw), "inventory|switch|serial|fabric"))
)
earliest=-24h@m
| eval serial_key=upper(trim(coalesce(serialNumber, serial_number, serial, deviceSerial, chassisSerial, serialNo, "")))
| eval dev_name=trim(coalesce(hostname, host, switch_name, deviceName, devicename, nodeName, switchName, switchId, ""))
| eval fab=trim(coalesce(fabric_name, fabricName, fabric, nd_fabric, lanFabric, ""))
| eval model_key=trim(coalesce(model, deviceModel, productId, pid, product_id, partNumber, ""))
| eval role_key=lower(trim(coalesce(switchRole, switch_role, role, nodeRole, node_role, "")))
| eval release_key=trim(coalesce(release, software_version, nxos_version, imageVersion, version, sw_version, runningImage, ""))
| eval ip_key=trim(coalesce(ipAddress, mgmt_ip, mgmtIp, management_ip, v4Addr, ipv4Addr, ""))
| eval managed_key=lower(trim(coalesce(managedStatus, managed_status, adminStatus, registrationState, onboardingState, "")))
| eval oper_key=lower(trim(coalesce(operStatus, oper_status, opStatus, reachability, connStatus, healthStatus, "")))
| where isnotnull(dev_name) AND dev_name!=""
| stats latest(_time) AS last_inv_poll latest(model_key) AS model latest(serial_key) AS serial latest(release_key) AS sw_version
        latest(ip_key) AS mgmt_ip latest(role_key) AS switch_role latest(managed_key) AS managed_status
        latest(oper_key) AS oper_status dc(fab) AS fabric_dc values(fab) AS fabric_list BY dev_name
| eval fabric_name=mvjoin(mvsort(fabric_list), " | ")
| lookup cisco_switch_eox_lookup model OUTPUT eos_date ldos_date pid_normalized AS lookup_pid
| eval days_to_ldos=if(isnotnull(ldos_date), round((strptime(ldos_date,"%Y-%m-%d")-now())/86400,1), null())
| eval serial_gap=if(isnull(serial) OR serial="" OR serial="UNKNOWN",1,0)
| eval reg_risk=case(
    managed_key IN ("unmanaged","pending","stale","unknown","unclaimed") OR match(managed_key,"false|no|not.?managed"), "registration_gap",
    oper_key IN ("down","unreachable","offline","major","critical") OR match(oper_key,"fail|loss"), "reachability_alert",
    serial_gap=1, "inventory_hole",
    1=1, "healthy")
| eval lifecycle_lane=case(isnull(ldos_date) AND isnotnull(model), "eox_lookup_miss",
    isnull(ldos_date), "no_model",
    days_to_ldos<0, "past_support_window",
    days_to_ldos<90, "ldos_within_90d",
    days_to_ldos<365, "ldos_within_1y",
    1=1, "eox_clear")
| where reg_risk!="healthy" OR lifecycle_lane IN ("eox_lookup_miss","past_support_window","ldos_within_90d","ldos_within_1y","no_model")
| eval sort_key=case(lifecycle_lane="past_support_window",0, lifecycle_lane="ldos_within_90d",1, lifecycle_lane="ldos_within_1y",2, reg_risk="reachability_alert",3, 1=1,4)
| sort sort_key days_to_ldos dev_name
| head 500
```

Understanding this SPL
• The coalesce() blocks absorb camelCase versus snake_case field churn across NDFC maintenance trains—append new vendor keys as they appear instead of cloning searches.
• stats latest(...) BY **dev_name** collapses every poll into a single row; **fabric_dc**>1 means the hostname appeared under multiple fabric scopes—resolve with architecture before exporting **CMDB** feeds.
• The lookup **cisco_switch_eox_lookup** step binds Cisco Last Date of Support style dates—rename OUTPUT fields if your CSV uses hardware_eos versus ldos_date.
• **reg_risk** separates controller registration health from EoX risk—page **registration_gap** before **reachability_alert** unless syslog proves a hard failure.
• **lifecycle_lane** encodes procurement thresholds; replace literal 90/365 boundaries with lookup-driven tiers when finance insists on different ladders.
• To compare running **NX-OS** against NDFC recommended releases, add a second lookup (for example **ndfc_recommended_release**); UC-18.4.10 remains the **imagePolicy** golden-compliance authority.
• Raise head 500 only after testing—all-large-fabric customers should add explicit **fabric_name** filters in dashboards to protect search concurrency.

Step 3 — Validate
(1) Count parity: compare `| stats dc(dev_name)` in Splunk and compare to the switch tally in the **NDFC LAN Fabric Inventory UI** for the same fabric—more than about two percent delta signals pagination or API filtering mistakes.
(2) Field spot-check: choose two known leaf switches; compare serial, model, and release to **NX-OS** CLI show inventory / show version bundles archived in **CMDB**.
(3) Staleness: | timechart span=1h count over **cisco:ndfc:inventory**; a flat line while the NDFC UI still updates means your collector—not the fabric—stopped.
(4) EoX sanity: pick a reference PID and compare computed **days_to_ldos** to the public **Cisco.com EoX** lookup; systematic mismatches indicate **pid_normalized** drift.
(5) Cross-source: when **operStatus** disagrees with ICMP, pivot to **cisco:ndfc:syslog** around the event time for supervisor or line-card transitions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• Dashboard layout: top row single values for **past_support_window**, **ldos_within_90d**, and **registration_gap**; middle table sorted by **sort_key**; bottom timechart of per-fabric inventory volume or **last_inv_poll** freshness.
• Scheduling: weekly CSV extracts for procurement, daily NetOps digests, immediate paging only when **past_support_window** intersects production fabric tokens documented in **CMDB**.
• **RBAC**: map Splunk roles to fabric abbreviations via search filters so tenant A operators never export tenant B serials.
• Runbooks: record who refreshes **cisco_switch_eox_lookup**, how ND bearer tokens rotate, and which **ServiceNow** change model covers refresh waves versus emergency **ISSU** events.
• Optional **ServiceNow** automation: trigger **CMDB** reconcile jobs when **inventory_hole** counts per site exceed your governance threshold.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• No **cisco:ndfc:inventory** events: validate the **/appcenter/cisco/ndfc** prefix for your release, curl the endpoint with the automation **Bearer token**, and grep **splunkd.log** for **REST TA** errors mentioning **inventory/switches**.
• Partial coverage: extend the poller to follow pagination tokens Cisco documents; if **HTTP 429** appears, lengthen the interval or shard fabrics across multiple inputs.
• Null **managed_status** columns: diff raw **JSON** against **props.conf** **FIELDALIAS** stanzas after every NDFC upgrade—camelCase renames are the usual culprit.
• Global EoX misses: normalize marketing prefixes (for example strip redundant N9K- tokens) inside props or an eval model=replace(...) before the lookup.
• Duplicate **dev_name** noise: enforce serial as the **CMDB** primary key in downstream exports when DNS reuse appears across labs and production.
• **fabric_list** cardinality: cap multivalue length or pivot to child events if dual-homed overlays explode dashboard width.
• **reachability_alert** noise during **ISSU**: join **ndfc_maintenance_allowlist** lookups keyed to **fabric_name** and **dev_name** with CHG identifiers.
• Indexer latency: set **SHOULD_LINEMERGE**=false for giant arrays and consider truncating oversized extracted fields at the forwarder if **KV_MODE=json** balloons _raw.
grandmaExplanation: We carry a living list of every switch your fabric controller says it owns—models, serials, and software ages—so renewal budgets and support deadlines stay honest before anything slips off contract quietly.
hardware: Nexus **93180YC-FX**, **9332C**, **9504**, **9508**, and other **NDFC**-managed **NX-OS** platforms in **VXLAN** **EVPN** fabrics; **Nexus Dashboard** cluster hosting **NDFC** **12.4.x** **LAN Fabric** application.
